item.title: Herbalife Revives Junk-Debt Sale After Shelving Deal Last Month
item.description: Herbalife Ltd. is taking another stab at selling junk debt, a month after shelving a loan offering due to market volatility.

item.title: I Was ‘Madame Due Process,’ Ex-OPEC Head Says At Bribery Trial
item.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke said she sought to increase transparency and competition in Nigeria’s oil industry while she was minister overseeing the sector at a London trial where she faces criminal charges of bribery.

item.title: BlackRock Sees Private Credit Tumult as Way to Take Market Share
item.description: BlackRock Inc. Chief Executive Officer Larry Fink sees increased demand for private credit from big institutional investors like insurers, even as retail clients grow skittish over the asset class and seek to redeem more of their shares.

item.title: Adnoc in Advanced Talks to Buy Shell’s South Africa Gas Stations
item.description: Shell Plc is in advanced talks with Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. to sell its retail fuel stations in South Africa, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Russia Struggles to Boost Oil Flows After Black Sea Port Hobbled
item.description: Another key site halted by drone strikes just as Baltic terminals ramp up

item.title: UK Faces Biggest Growth and Price Shock from Iran War, IMF Says
item.description: Britain will suffer the biggest economic shock from the Iran war of any major advanced economy and struggle to cushion the blow through either interest rate cuts or state support, according to the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Lightspeed’s Mignano Joins Union Square Ventures to Make AI Bets
item.description: Lightspeed Venture Partners investor Michael Mignano, notable for bets on consumer-facing artificial intelligence companies, is leaving the firm to join New York-based Union Square Ventures.

item.title: IMF Cut Global Growth to 3.1%, Flags Downturn Risk If War Lasts
item.description: The International Monetary Fund downgraded its growth projection for the year after the war in the Middle East triggered a major oil shock and included the possibility of a downturn if the conflict drags on and energy infrastructure is severally damaged.

item.title: TCW Private Credit Fund Slashes Red Lobster Equity Value by 98%
item.description: TCW Group has marked down its equity stake in Red Lobster by roughly 98% since acquiring it via the restaurant’s 2024 bankruptcy, leaving shares held by a private credit fund it oversees now worth less than $1 million, according to a recent regulatory filing.

item.title: Japan Exchanges Eye Call-Option ETFs to Attract Global Investors
item.description: Japan’s two major exchanges are looking to expand exchange-traded fund products including ones that offer covered calls to attract new investors.

item.title: Stock Futures Extend Gains After Cooler Producer Price Data
item.description: Stock futures extended gains Tuesday morning after US wholesale prices rose less than expected in March despite a rise in energy prices resulting from the Iran war.

item.title: Citigroup Reports $22 Billion of Private-Credit Exposure
item.description: Citigroup Inc. said its exposure to private-credit firms was $22 billion in the first quarter with zero losses, offering a look at what has become a closely watched development by investors.

item.title: Ship Testing Hormuz Blockade Appears to Transit Then U-Turn
item.description: A US-sanctioned tanker linked to China that was being watched as one of the first vessels to test President Donald Trump’s naval blockade appeared to U-turn after sailing out of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Carlyle Draws $1.5 Billion for Asset-Backed Fund in First Round
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. raised $1.5 billion in its first round of fundraising for a new vehicle investing in asset-backed finance as the private equity giant grows its credit business.

item.title: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
item.description: The Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing next week for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, the panel’s top Republican said Tuesday.

item.title: Dollar and VIX Are Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
item.description: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

item.title: Alibaba Readies First Robot for Foray Into Crowded Chinese Arena
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. plans to soon unveil its first robot, a four-legged device that will mark its entry into an increasingly crowded but popular arena.

item.title: Iran War Lifts Africa Debt Costs That Have Surged Since Pandemic
item.description: The Iran war is raising borrowing costs for African countries that are only just recovering from the post-pandemic shock that sent debt burdens soaring.

item.title: EU to Bet on Electricification to Avert Recurring Energy Crises
item.description: The European Union will float a series of measures next week to help the continent deal with the Iran war’s energy price fallout, as well as electrify the economy to avert future fossil fuel shocks.

item.title: KKR Limits Redemptions From Asset-Based Fund After 7.2% Sought Withdrawals
item.description: KKR & Co. has capped withdrawals at 5% of its asset-based finance fund K-ABF, after the vehicle saw redemption requests of roughly 7.2%.

item.title: Amazon to Buy Satellite Operator Globalstar for $90 a Share
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. agreed to acquire satellite operator Globalstar Inc. in a roughly $11.6 billion deal that would boost the tech giant’s efforts to build its own satellite operation.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: American Airlines, Dell, Credo, Lucid
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.2% as of 7:51 a.m. in New York on optimism that the US and Iran are considering another round of peace talks.

item.title: Citi Logs Best Returns in Five Years as Revamp Takes Hold
item.description: Citigroup Inc. traders rode a wave of volatility to push the Wall Street bank to its highest quarterly revenue in a decade, notching another success for Chief Executive Officer Jane Fraser’s turnaround plan.

item.title: Merz Says EU €90 Billion Loan for Kyiv Should Be Disbursed Now
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said a European Union €90 billion ($106 billion) loan for Ukraine must be disbursed rapidly after an electoral defeat for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban eliminated a major roadblock to the funding.

item.title: Property Mogul Targets Top Texas Finance Job With Anti-Tax Pitch
item.description: The Texas comptroller’s office, with its spreadsheets and spending reports, doesn’t typically draw ideological firebrands. But Don Huffines doesn’t plan on being a typical Texas comptroller.

item.title: Iran Weighs Pausing Hormuz Shipping to Avoid Derailing Talks
item.description: Iran is considering a short-term pause to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz to avoid testing a US blockade and scuppering a fresh round of peace talks, according to a person familiar with Tehran’s deliberations.

item.title: UK’s Reeves Calls Out Folly of US War in Iran Without Exit Plan
item.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves criticized US President Donald Trump over the damage his conflict in the Middle East has wreaked on the global economy and accused him of going to war without an exit strategy.

item.title: Warning Sign for Stocks Seen in Surging Inflation Expectations
item.description: Soaring inflation expectations are a warning sign for investors who have bought into the ceasefire-fueled rebound in stocks over the past few trading sessions, say some Wall Street market-watchers.

item.title: China’s Teapot Refiners Fight to Survive Deepening Oil Crisis
item.description: The country’s independent crude processors rely heavily on discounted Iranian, Russian and Venezuelan crude, putting them in a perilous position.

item.title: Macron Leads EU Push to Coordinate Social Media Bans for Minors
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and a group of European Union leaders pushing to restrict social media use among minors are set to meet remotely on Thursday to coordinate their efforts.
